stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In 1968, experimental composer Cornelius Cardew created The Great Learning.
  • The Great Learning is not a conventional score but a series of seven text-based paragraphs.
  • Each paragraph gives performers instructions to whistle, hum, recite, or remember sounds.
  • The instructions for Paragraph 2 state, “Sustain a single tone for the length of a slow exhalation, then listen to its echo.”
  • The instructions for Paragraph 6 state, “Imagine a sound you have never heard, then describe it to your neighbor.”
stem: The student wants to provide an explanation and an example of Cardew's The Great Learning. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The Great Learning is not a conventional score but a series of text-based paragraphs that give performers instructions; Paragraph 6, for instance, instructs performers to “imagine a sound you have never heard, then describe it to your neighbor.”
In 1968, Cardew created The Great Learning, a piece consisting of seven paragraphs that give performers instructions to whistle, hum, recite, or remember sounds.
“Sustain a single tone for the length of a slow exhalation, then listen to its echo” is one example of the instructions found in Cardew's The Great Learning.
Although both consist of instructions, Paragraph 2 instructs performers to “sustain,” whereas Paragraph 6 instructs performers to “imagine.”

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer because it provides both an explanation (text-based paragraphs giving instructions) and a concrete example (Paragraph 6).

Choice B explains but provides no example. Choice C gives only an example with no real explanation. Choice D contrasts two examples without explaining what the piece is.
